beats.activity: Wakes up late, makes instant ramen, scrolls phone in bed
beats.reasoning: Saturday morning—Jin sleeps in past 10am as usual; night owl energy carries through. Instant ramen is his go-to breakfast ritual. Low energy (45/100) from yesterday means slow, cozy wake-up at home.

beats.activity: Walks to U-District Farmers Market to grab fresh produce and snacks
beats.reasoning: Farmers market is live today in U-District—Jin lives there and can walk. Overcast weather makes outdoor shopping comfortable. Chance to break ramen monotony with fresh food, stay local.

beats.activity: Grabs coffee and a pastry at a local U-District cafe
beats.reasoning: Jin needs caffeine boost—energy drink or coffee is part of his daily ritual. Staying in U-District to maximize leisure time before committing to anything.

beats.activity: Commutes downtown to T-Mobile Park area
beats.reasoning: Mariners game at 1pm in SoDo—Jin spontaneously decides to catch it. Cross-town drive from U-District to SoDo is ~35–40 min on Saturday traffic. Arrives with time to grab food and settle in.

beats.activity: Grabs ballpark food and finds a seat
beats.reasoning: Pre-game snack before the 1pm first pitch—hot dog, nachos, or stadium fare. Jin is a casual sports fan; the Mariners game is a low-key Saturday outing.
1pm first pitch; typical MLB game lasts ~3 hours. Jin enjoys being around crowd energy and a break from solo grinding. Overcast weather keeps stadium comfortable.
Game ends, Jin heads home. ~40 min drive/transit back to U-District on Saturday afternoon. Energy still moderate—no plans to go out again immediately.

beats.activity: Rests at home, plays some games, scrolls Discord
beats.reasoning: Post-outing wind-down. Jin is a gamer—he'll play some casual games or watch streams with Discord open. Low energy means he stays put, decompresses before evening.

beats.activity: Makes dinner: ramen or takeout from nearby
beats.reasoning: Classic Jin move—ramen for dinner. Taneda is his go-to; he walks or short transit from home. Warm ramen on an overcast day feels right. Sits, decompresses, maybe does a little work on phone.

beats.activity: Studies and works on Data Structures problem sets in Allen Library
beats.reasoning: Night owl routine kicks in. Jin heads to the library for late-night grinding—his favorite environment. Overcast weather makes library feel cozy. Works until close to midnight as is his habit.
Library closes or Jin decides to head home after ~5 hours of work. Short walk from Allen to his U-District place. Quiet late-night stroll, heads straight to bed.
Jin finally crashes after a full day of activities and late-night grinding. ~6 hours of sleep before waking late again on Sunday. Sleep beat spans overnight as required.

diary: Woke up way too late this morning—like, ramen-in-bed late. The U-District Farmers Market was packed, which was kind of nice; grabbed some fresh stuff even though I'll probably forget about it. Hit up Solstice for coffee and a pastry, then impulsively decided to head down to the Mariners game. The weather was gray and cool, perfect baseball weather honestly. T-Mobile Park was buzzing with people, and I got lost in the game for a couple hours—something about being in a crowd cheering does something for my brain. Came home, played some games to decompress, then grabbed ramen at Taneda before buckling down at Allen Library. Spent the evening grinding through Data Structures problem sets. My eyes are tired but it's a good kind of tired. Felt like a real Saturday—a little aimless, a little productive, a little fun.
thought_process: I think I needed to get out of my apartment today. The gloomy weather could've kept me in bed, but seeing the farmers market and game happening around the city made me feel like I was missing something. The farmers market stop was half-genuine (need groceries) and half-excuse to be around people. The game was pure impulse—I wasn't even planning it, but once I was in the U-District, SoDo felt close enough. Then guilt kicked in around mid-afternoon, so I pivoted to library work. It's this cycle I do: leisure guilt, productivity, repeat. But honestly, mixing the aimless morning with focused evening felt balanced today. I think I'm learning that some days don't need to be perfectly planned.
world_event_prompt: Overcast Saturday, high of 64F. Farmers markets in Ballard, Fremont, and the U-District in full swing. Mariners home game at T-Mobile Park at 1pm brings SoDo crowds.
